summary: Most parasitic infections involve hepatocytes at some stage; however, *Leishmania* does not predominantly reside within these cells as they mainly infect macrophages instead. The primary site for development varies significantly between different parasite types including those affecting liver tissue directly versus indirectly through other cell lines like macrophages where Leishmania thrives initially before spreading elsewhere if necessary conditions persist post-infection initiation phase inside hepatic counterparts only briefly possibly transiently. To be precise though, Leishmania doesn't extensively occupy hepatocyte spaces unlike many common helminths or protozoans which do so more consistently throughout various stages of
